title: Add interruption and resume regression coverage for transaction checkpoints
summary: Add the 0.6.0 regression coverage that proves the redesigned work loop survives a single interruption at canonical transaction checkpoints and resumes deterministically on first continuation. This ticket focuses on checkpoint-oriented interruption scenarios such as interrupted in-progress work, interrupted verification flow, interrupted commit flow, and interrupted post-commit pre-terminal completion. The goal is to verify that canonical transaction state, append-only event history, and `next_action` together are sufficient to continue the exact active transaction without heuristic reconstruction or duplicate logical completion, while leaving repeated-resume idempotence to the separate follow-up coverage ticket.
outputs: Regression tests for interruption at canonical transaction checkpoints
Coverage that proves resume continues the exact active transaction after interruption
Coverage that proves healthy materialized state is the primary resume entrypoint and rebuild is the fallback when needed
Coverage that proves `next_action` remains sufficient to continue interrupted work through key lifecycle checkpoints
Coverage that proves interrupted checkpoint continuation does not duplicate logical completion or invent replacement transactions
Documented test expectations for interruption-safe behavior across major non-terminal checkpoint states
acceptance_criteria: Regression coverage exists for interruption during `in-progress` work before verification begins.
Regression coverage exists for interruption around verification checkpoints, including at least interrupted verify-start and interrupted verify-pass or verify-fail continuation scenarios.
Regression coverage exists for interruption around commit checkpoints, including at least interrupted commit-start and interrupted commit-done continuation scenarios.
Regression coverage exists for interruption after successful repository commit but before explicit terminal end handling.
The coverage is centered on first-resume behavior after a checkpoint interruption and does not serve as the primary ticket for repeated-resume idempotence semantics.
The tests prove that resume continues the exact active non-terminal transaction rather than inventing or selecting a replacement transaction candidate.
The tests prove that terminal transactions are not resumed as active work after interruption.
The tests prove that healthy materialized transaction state is used as the primary resume anchor and that documented rebuild fallback behavior remains compatible with interruption recovery.
The tests prove that canonical `next_action` is sufficient to guide continuation through the covered interruption scenarios.
The tests prove that interruption recovery does not duplicate logical completion across verify, commit, or post-commit continuation boundaries.

The tests are aligned with the 0.6.0 transaction-centered resumable protocol and do not depend on ticket-derived transaction identity, sentinel active-transaction semantics, or heuristic resume selection.
The regression coverage preserves canonical lifecycle ordering, replay integrity, resumability, and explicit terminal-state handling.
tasks: Identify the canonical checkpoint scenarios that must be covered to validate interruption-safe continuation of the redesigned 0.6.0 work loop.
Add tests for interruption during `in-progress` work before verification begins.
Add tests for interruption during verification-oriented checkpoints, including interrupted verify-start and interrupted verify result handling where appropriate.
Add tests for interruption during commit-oriented checkpoints, including interrupted commit-start and interrupted commit completion handling.
Add tests for interruption after repository commit but before explicit terminal end handling so commit remains non-terminal in practice.
Add tests that verify first resume after interruption continues the exact active non-terminal transaction across the covered interruption checkpoints.
Add tests that verify canonical `next_action` remains sufficient to continue the interrupted active transaction through each covered scenario.
Add tests that verify terminal transactions are not resumed as active work after interruption or repeat entry.
Add tests that verify interruption recovery does not duplicate logical completion or mint replacement transaction identities during continuation.
Add or update fixtures and helper utilities as needed to simulate interruption at materialized-state and event-history checkpoint boundaries without weakening realism.
Document any bounded assumptions in the tests so future changes do not silently reintroduce heuristic resume behavior or commit-equals-success semantics.
notes: This ticket is regression-hardening work and should be implemented after the core 0.6.0 runtime semantics are in place.
The most important outcome is confidence that a single interruption at key checkpoints does not break deterministic first-resume continuation of the active transaction.
The tests should focus on canonical checkpoint behavior and exact transaction continuation rather than overfitting internal implementation details.
The tests should make it difficult for future changes to silently collapse the distinction between commit completion and terminal transaction success.
Repeated-resume and idempotent continuation semantics belong primarily to `p3-t02`, not this ticket.
The tests should reinforce the transaction-centered resume model by proving that exact active transaction identity plus `next_action` is enough to continue interrupted work.

coverage_focus.primary_goal: Prove that a single interruption at canonical checkpoints still allows deterministic first-resume continuation of the exact active transaction.
coverage_focus.non_goal: Exhaustively test every internal branch that does not affect checkpoint-oriented interruption semantics or serve as the main idempotent repeated-resume coverage.

required_coverage: terminal re-entry does not resume active work
checkpoint_scenarios.reason: These scenarios collectively validate that the redesigned work loop behaves like a resumable transaction protocol across its most important lifecycle checkpoints.

resume_assertions.exact_identity: First resume after interruption must continue the same active non-terminal transaction identity across covered checkpoint scenarios.
resume_assertions.next_action: Canonical `next_action` must remain sufficient to direct first continuation after interruption.
resume_assertions.non_terminal_commit: Commit completion must remain non-terminal until explicit end handling occurs.
resume_assertions.no_duplicate_completion: Interruption recovery must not duplicate logical verify, commit, or terminal completion.

persistence_assertions.primary_anchor: Healthy materialized transaction state should act as the primary resume entrypoint.
persistence_assertions.fallback_anchor: Canonical event-history rebuild should remain the fallback when materialized state is missing or incomplete.
persistence_assertions.integrity_expectation: Malformed or ambiguous canonical persistence should fail clearly rather than silently guess a continuation target.

anti_regression_targets: sentinel active-transaction semantics in normal flow
commit treated as implicit terminal success
terminal transactions being resumed as active work

design_bias.preferred_anchor: Checkpoint-oriented interruption tests that assert exact active transaction continuation and explicit terminal handling on first resume after interruption.
avoid: tests that depend primarily on incidental implementation details
tests that blur the distinction between commit and terminal success
tests that assume heuristic recovery is acceptable
tests that use this ticket as the primary place to specify repeated-resume idempotence semantics
tests that reintroduce planning-artifact status as canonical continuation truth

canonical_alignment.synchronization_intent: Use this ticket to lock in interruption-safe checkpoint continuation on first resume as a durable property of the 0.6.0 resumable transaction protocol.
canonical_alignment.current_canonical_constraint: Regression coverage must validate exact active-transaction continuation, checkpoint-oriented resumability, non-terminal commit semantics, and explicit terminal completion without reintroducing heuristic recovery behavior, while leaving repeated-resume idempotence coverage primarily to `p3-t02`.
